title: Lepisorus superficialis

Lepisorus superficialis belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. The species is also classified as a type of terrestrial fern, indicating its ability to grow on land. Lepisorus superficialis is native to certain regions, including parts of Asia.

intro: Lepisorus superficialis, commonly known as the superficial lepidosorus fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Lepisorus superficialis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
